changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_log_transfer_to_external_storage_enabled to k8s_audit_log_external_storage_transfer_enabled for consistency and clarity
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to audit logging, which is a key component of K8s security. The original function names were mostly accurate but required slight modification for consistency with naming conventions. The change made enhances clarity by aligning with the typical K8s function naming pattern.
naming_improvements: Use 'external_storage_transfer' instead of 'transfer_to_external_storage' for better alignment with K8s naming conventions
title: Audit Log Storage Capacity - Audit log storage capacity | transfer to alternate storage transfer audit logs [
step1_reasoning: The control AU-4(1) focuses on ensuring sufficient audit log storage capacity and the ability to transfer logs to alternate storage. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logs are configured with adequate storage capacity, that there is a mechanism to transfer logs to external storage (e.g., centralized logging solutions), and that retention policies are defined to manage log lifecycle.
